evidence_snippets: we’re aiming for a 60-second auto-refresh
coverage_entries.reason: Sources explicitly require ~60 second auto-refresh/polling.

evidence_snippets: stick to the DHS color palette
coverage_entries.reason: Multiple artifacts mandate using the DHS color palette.

evidence_snippets: read the event/activity fields at about 30 feet
coverage_entries.reason: Sources require projection legibility at ~30 ft for most viewers.

evidence_snippets: near 99 percent during normal training/business hours
coverage_entries.reason: Documents specify target ~99% availability during normal business hours.

evidence_snippets: at ~100 ft the 'now' state should still be obvious
coverage_entries.reason: Sources require the now indicator to be obvious from far back.

evidence_snippets: must not show data to people who aren’t authorized internally
coverage_entries.reason: Wallboard must require sign-in and prevent unauthorized data viewing.

evidence_snippets: get the board up ... in well under two hours
coverage_entries.reason: Documents expect intuitive UI and quick setup by new users.

evidence_snippets: keep minute-level updates snappy
coverage_entries.reason: Responsiveness and smooth minute-level updates are explicitly required.

evidence_snippets: split workspace dual-pane wallboard
coverage_entries.reason: Sources specify a split view showing timeline (graph) and table.

evidence_snippets: right panel = vertical table (time-ordered)
coverage_entries.reason: Multiple notes require a vertical, time-ordered right-side table.

evidence_snippets: split view: graph and list side by side
coverage_entries.reason: Interview and notes require a time-based graph/timeline.

evidence_snippets: show both Events and Activities
coverage_entries.reason: Text explicitly states timeline must show Events and Activities.

evidence_snippets: left two-thirds timeline; right one-third table
coverage_entries.reason: Multiple sources specify left 2/3 graphical, right 1/3 table.

evidence_snippets: display the MSEL Summary fields
coverage_entries.reason: Devon and notes state wallboard shows MSEL Summary data fields.

evidence_snippets: Right-side table split: sequential and temporal
coverage_entries.reason: Notes and interview define table split into sequence and temporal.

evidence_snippets: top quarter reserved for sequence-driven items
coverage_entries.reason: Meeting notes explicitly assign top 1/4 to sequence items.

evidence_snippets: bottom 3/4 shows items near current time
coverage_entries.reason: Sources describe lower three-quarters showing near-now relevant items.

evidence_snippets: Use color to reflect variance (ahead/on-time/behind)
coverage_entries.reason: Stakeholders require color coding for schedule variance within palette.

evidence_snippets: pull from Sync Matrix 1.0 and EMT 1.0
coverage_entries.reason: Documents state data sources are Sync Matrix 1.0 and EMT 1.0.

evidence_snippets: items should link back to the exact source record
coverage_entries.reason: Multiple artifacts require deep links to source apps for edits.

evidence_snippets: pick which columns show
Allow limited column show/hide
coverage_entries.reason: Users can tweak/hide columns and perform light display customization.

evidence_snippets: All Events view
display of all the Events in the exercise
coverage_entries.reason: Sources explicitly request an All-Events view showing all events.

evidence_snippets: venue filter, Key Events toggle
Views: per-venue views, Key Events-only view
coverage_entries.reason: Specifies venue filtering and a Key Events toggle/filter.

evidence_snippets: user-defined time segments
Users could define morning, lunch, afternoon
coverage_entries.reason: Documents user-defined time segments and configurable grid granularity.

evidence_snippets: two clocks on screen: local time and exercise time
Show local time and exercise time simultaneously
coverage_entries.reason: Multiple sources require separate local and exercise clocks.

evidence_snippets: Pause and Resume
Pause/Resume control with a clear indicator
coverage_entries.reason: Sources require pausing/resuming auto-refresh with visible paused state.

evidence_snippets: simple chart comparing actual vs planned times
include a basic chart view for actual vs planned times
coverage_entries.reason: Chart for actual versus planned times is explicitly requested.

evidence_snippets: change history view listing modifications
surface history from the source apps
coverage_entries.reason: Sources request a change history report of observed/source changes.

unsupported_implied.text: The timeline shall offer selectable grid granularity (e.g., 5, 15, 30 minute intervals) so users can change how time is binned/displayed.
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: "pick the time window or granularity—like 5, 15, or 30 minute grid"
unsupported_implied.reason: The gold set requires allowing user-defined time segments but does not require adjustable timeline grid/granularity options. The source explicitly asks for selectable 5/15/30 minute granularity, which is a distinct UI capability not covered by the gold requirements.

unsupported_implied.text: The application shall be a single-window split-pane UI only (no pop-ups) — i.e., a single-window MDI with two fixed regions (left/right) and no separate pop-up windows.
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: "one window with two regions; avoid pop-ups."
unsupported_implied.reason: The gold requires an MDI form showing graph and table but does not forbid pop-ups or require a single-window-only constraint. The source explicitly mandates a single-window split view and forbids pop-ups, which is an additional UI constraint not present in the gold set.

unsupported_implied.text: Pause must present a clear visible paused state (e.g., a ribbon/banner) and suspend polling; Resume must fetch the latest data and re-synchronize the views to current exercise time.
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: "Pause/Resume; when paused, visibly indicate paused state; on resume, sync to current exercise time."
unsupported_implied.reason: While the gold requires the ability to pause and resume refresh, it does not require a clearly visible paused indicator nor specify that resume must re-fetch and align the display to exercise time. The source explicitly requires both the visible paused state and the resume re-synchronization behavior, which are concrete functional behaviors beyond the gold wording.

unsupported_implied.text: The lower (near-now) portion of the right-side table shall present items within a defined proximity window by default (approximately +/-30 minutes) for the 'near now' view.
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: "Proximity window defaults around +/- 30 minutes [TBD if adjustable]."
unsupported_implied.reason: The gold requires the table split into sequential and temporal regions and that the bottom 3/4 hold near-current items, but it does not specify a default proximity window size. The source specifies a concrete default of +/-30 minutes for the proximity window, which is an operational requirement absent from the gold set.

unsupported_implied.text: The change history view shall include timestamps and, where the source provides it, the identity of who made the change (i.e., surface actor metadata when available).
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: "change history would help—seeing what changed and when during the day" / "timestamps and who made the change when that information is provided by the sources."
unsupported_implied.reason: The gold requires a history report of changes but does not require inclusion of actor/identity metadata. The source explicitly requires showing who made changes when that information is available, which is an additional traceability detail not covered by the gold requirements.
